# Course Spec: Intro to Widget Systems

## Topic
How small mechanical widgets combine into a working assembly line.

## Scope
- **In:** widget anatomy, tolerances, assembly-line sequencing, failure diagnosis.
- **Out:** widget manufacturing/materials science, factory-floor safety regulations.

## Audience
- **Who:** a hobbyist who has built individual widgets but never a full line.
- **Assumed prior knowledge:** basic hand tools, reading a simple schematic.

## Depth & size
- **Phases:** 3 (fundamentals, assembly, diagnosis)
- **Lessons (approx):** 6

## Running example / project backbone
A single running example: **"MiniLine"**, a 4-station tabletop assembly line built and refined
across every lesson.

## Optional modules
- [x] Hands-on exercises / katas
- [ ] Diagrams
- [ ] Socratic teaching

## Source material
- **Ground against:** the fixture's own placeholder references (this is a test fixture, not a
  real course).
- **Avoid:** nothing specific — test fixture.

## Notes / open questions
None — this fixture is deliberately well-formed for the intake happy path.
